extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD won by 1:38 over Pogačar, pacing aggressively from the gun while Pogačar lost time before his bike change on the final climb.
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD started significantly faster on flat opening sections, building 30-second margin before climb began—opposite typical TT pacing strategy.
POGAČAR lost critical time pre-bike change, suggesting poor equipment choice kept him on TT bike too long into climb transition.
CICCONE intentionally soft-pedaled flat sections to save energy for climbing points, prioritizing KOM over stage result with split-effort strategy.
Multiple favorites including KUNG made bike changes for final climb, but timing/execution separated winners from losers significantly.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: First World Tour TT win by nearly 2-minute margin suggests previous TT results massively underrated his ability against clock
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: negative: Lost 1:38 despite second place indicates TT form significantly declined from prior Tours or tactical errors on bike change timing
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Moved onto podium via TT performance, showing unexpected time trialing strength for pure climber type
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Carlos: negative: Lost podium spot by only 5 seconds, suggesting GC threat diminished by TT weakness
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: neutral: Split-effort strategy for KOM points reveals climbers can game stage formats when GC not priority
